actions: Courts noblewoman connected to powerful figure in home city.
Accepts diplomatic exile rather than face punishment.
Travels to foreign lands by caravan with mentor.
Takes refuge with foreigners after raiders attack caravan.
Witnesses foreign ruler's funeral and ritual sacrifice.
Observes leader kill rival in self-defense, establishing succession.
Reluctantly accepts selection into warband by oracle's prophecy.
Prepares foreign mount for journey despite warriors' mockery.
Learns foreign language through patient observation around campfire.
demon/monsterstrates skilled mount handling to silence mocking warriors.
Teaches leader to write in foreign script at leader's request.
Declines drink violating religious/government law, accepts permitted alternative.
Travels by sea and land with warband to besieged fortification/settlement.
Searches raided dwelling and discovers enemy's cult icon.
Fights in hall during first enemy attack, surviving the night.
Reforges foreign weapon into familiar cultural style.
Reasons out assault strategy through indirect question to companion.
Fights in multiple defensive clashes against enemy raiders.
Rides out during battle to rescue civilian from enemy.
Swims through underwater passages to infiltrate enemy stronghold.
Fights through enemy lair alongside surviving companions.
Escapes enemy lair after cult leader killed, leaving fallen companion.
Returns to settlement to prepare fortifications for last stand.
Prays for forgiveness before final battle.
Joins foreign warriors in reciting their death prayer.
Fights in final battle as enemy assault is broken.
Witnesses leader's death and funeral alongside survivors.
Departs for home, vowing to record leader's deeds.
Receives education in letters and verse as a child.
Trains in athletics and mount handling during youth.
Follows father into civic magistracy in home city.
Consults oracle about how to gain renown.
Debases state currency through fraud or corruption.
Flees or is exiled after scandal is discovered.
Travels to great city as exile and stranger.
Derives philosophical lesson from observing simple animal.
Seeks out harsh teacher despite repeated refusal.
Subjects body to extreme heat as hardening exercise.
Subjects body to extreme cold as hardening exercise.
Goes barefoot through city as ascetic discipline.
Begs in public spaces.
Discards possession after seeing simpler alternative.
Practices receiving refusal by begging from inanimate object.
Performs shocking bodily act publicly to make philosophical point.
Answers insult with deliberate bodily degradation of insulter.
Insults wealthy host's dignity while accepting hospitality.
Lures crowd with promise, then punishes them for failing test.
Publicly shames attackers until they are disgraced.
Attempts extreme ascetic practice but fails physically.
Moves against crowd flow as deliberate life practice.
Tramples rival philosopher's luxury, denouncing vanity.
Demands concrete proof of rival's abstract philosophical concepts.
Tests rival's appetite, then mocks resulting hypocrisy.
Positions self near danger to mock incompetent practitioner.
Refutes abstract argument through demon/monsterstrative action.
Disrupts pompous speech with distracting mundane act.
Mocks religious worship through satirical dedication.
Defaces monument with inscription critiquing its source.
Annotates public inscription with cynical observation.
Refuses religious initiation, preferring virtue to afterlife promise.
Retaliates violently after being struck unprovoked.
Counsels fellow captives to accept fate with philosophical calm.
Selects own buyer at slave auction, claiming to be master.
Takes charge of owner's household and children's education as slave.
Teaches owner's children martial and athletic skills.
Trains children in self-sufficiency and simple living.
Crowns self victor at games with improvised wreath.
Reframes captivity as surveillance upon captor's vice; is released.
Mocks powerful figure's death threat with contemptuous comparison.
Insults messenger with wordplay on messenger's own name.
Adopts walking stick as concession to age while traveling.
Mocks admired people about their failures despite respecting them.
Instructs that corpse be given to nature without ceremony.
Quarrels with foster-brother ruler, exiled for treason.
Writes book chronicling explorations through foreign wilderness.
Enters rival nation's service as advisor to ruler.
Assists ruler during perilous sorcerous/magical conjuring.
Fights summoned horror during ritual, hewing regenerating limbs.
Helps complete sorcery/magic, banishing enemy hero to cursed prison.
Reports to noblewoman on battle aftermath and court intrigues.
Advises ruler on strategy, warns against trusting rival general.
Serves as noblewoman's escort at court functions.
Sails with invasion fleet to enemy homeland.
Travels alone, encounters strange wonders in wilderness.
Betrays own side, helps beloved escape after siege.
Fights alongside beloved against pursuers until rescue arrives.
Defects to former enemy's cause, joins their forces.

Kills new ally in response to accusations, reverts to original side.
